Frost exposure (0.0% of hours at or below freezing) is within the ideal band (0–0.5) for Oil Seeds. Rainfall (172 mm (annualized)) is within the ideal band (150–1400) for Oil Seeds. Soil pH (7.53) is within the ideal band (5–8.5) for Oil Seeds. Soil organic carbon (10.2 g/kg) is within the ideal band (4–50) for Oil Seeds. Wind speed (13.6 km/h) is within the ideal band (0–40) for Oil Seeds. (+1 more positives)

llm_reasoning: Suitability is poor primarily due to extremely low annual rainfall (307.33 mm), far below the 400-700 mm required by most oilseed crops, making severe water stress the strongest limiting factor. The strongest positive is the absence of frost risk and a moderate mean temperature (14.89°C), which are favorable. Operational risks include critical water scarcity necessitating extensive irrigation, potential for wind damage to taller varieties, and managing low soil organic carbon. Mitigation requires robust irrigation systems, drought-tolerant varieties, windbreaks, and soil organic matter enhancement.
